title: Grocery Delivery App — Initial Build

requirements.text: Design and apply a fresh, vibrant visual theme inspired by farmers' markets and fresh produce — using warm greens, earthy neutrals, and crisp whites as the color palette, with clean sans-serif typography, card-based layouts, and subtle produce-inspired accent illustrations. Apply this theme consistently across all pages.
acceptanceCriteria: A consistent color palette of greens, earthy neutrals, and whites is used site-wide.
Typography is clean and readable across all screen sizes.
Cards are used to display products and categories consistently.

requirements.text: Create a backend data model and persistence layer for grocery products, including fields: id, name, category, description, price (in cents), imageUrl, stock quantity, and availability flag.
acceptanceCriteria: Products are stored in stable backend state.
Each product has id, name, category, description, price, imageUrl, stock, and isAvailable fields.
Products can be queried by category or retrieved as a full list.

requirements.text: Seed the backend with at least 20 sample grocery products across at least 5 categories (e.g., Fruits & Vegetables, Dairy, Bakery, Beverages, Snacks).
acceptanceCriteria: At least 20 products exist in the backend on first deploy.
Products span at least 5 distinct categories.
Each product has valid price, stock, and category values.

requirements.text: Build a homepage with a hero banner, a featured categories section, and a 'Featured Products' grid showing top products. Include a persistent navigation bar with the app name/logo, a search bar, and a cart icon showing the current item count.
acceptanceCriteria: The homepage renders a hero banner with a tagline and a call-to-action button.
Category cards are displayed and clickable to filter products.
A featured products grid shows at least 6 products.
The navigation bar includes logo, search bar, and cart icon with item count badge.

requirements.text: Build a product browse/catalog page that displays all products in a responsive grid. Support filtering by category and sorting by price (low-to-high, high-to-low). Each product card must show name, image, price, and an 'Add to Cart' button.
acceptanceCriteria: All products are displayed in a responsive grid (2–4 columns depending on screen size).
Category filter narrows visible products correctly.
Sort by price works in both ascending and descending order.
Each product card shows name, image placeholder, price, and Add to Cart button.
Adding a product to cart updates the cart icon count in the navbar.

requirements.text: Implement a client-side shopping cart (stored in React state) that allows users to add products, increase/decrease quantities, remove items, and view a running subtotal. Display the cart as a slide-in drawer accessible from the cart icon in the navbar.
acceptanceCriteria: The cart drawer opens when the cart icon is clicked.
Users can increase or decrease item quantity or remove items.
Subtotal updates in real-time as quantities change.
The cart icon badge reflects the total number of items in the cart.

requirements.text: Create a backend order model and persistence layer. An order must include: orderId, buyerName, deliveryAddress, items (list of productId + quantity), totalAmount, status (pending/confirmed/delivered), and createdAt timestamp. Expose a function to place a new order and a function to retrieve all orders.
acceptanceCriteria: Orders are stored in stable backend state.
Each order contains all required fields.
A placeOrder function accepts buyer info, address, and cart items and persists the order.
A getOrders function returns all stored orders.

requirements.text: Build a checkout page where users enter their name, delivery address, and a delivery time preference. On submission, call the backend to place the order and show an order confirmation screen with an order summary and estimated delivery message.
acceptanceCriteria: Checkout form includes name, delivery address (street, city, zip), and delivery time fields.
Form validates that all required fields are filled before submission.
On successful submission, the order is persisted in the backend.
A confirmation screen shows the order summary, total, and a friendly estimated delivery message.
The cart is cleared after a successful order.

requirements.text: Build a simple admin/orders page (accessible via a nav link) that fetches and displays all placed orders in a table, showing order ID, buyer name, delivery address, item count, total amount, and status.
acceptanceCriteria: The orders page fetches all orders from the backend.
Each order row displays id, buyer name, address, item count, total, and status.
The table is readable and styled consistently with the app theme.

requirements.text: Render the generated app hero banner image and category/product placeholder images as static assets from frontend/public/assets/generated.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero banner image is loaded from /assets/generated/hero-banner.png.
Category images are loaded from /assets/generated/ using their respective filenames.
No broken image links appear on any page.

constraints: All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo.
Shopping cart state must be managed client-side (React state); do not persist cart to backend.
Do not use any external databases or cloud services.
Immutable frontend paths (hooks, main.tsx, ui components) must not be modified.
nonGoals: User authentication or login (no Internet Identity in this build).
Real-time order tracking or WebSocket updates.
Admin product management UI (adding/editing products via UI).

required: Bright, appetizing hero banner with an assortment of fresh colorful vegetables and fruits on a light background with warm green accents (hero-banner.dim_1200x400.png)
Category tile illustration for Fruits & Vegetables — a colorful flat-style produce basket (cat-fruits-veggies.dim_400x300.png)
Category tile illustration for Dairy — minimalist flat-style milk bottle and cheese (cat-dairy.dim_400x300.png)
Category tile illustration for Bakery — warm flat-style bread loaf and pastries (cat-bakery.dim_400x300.png)
Category tile illustration for Beverages — flat-style juice bottles and a coffee cup (cat-beverages.dim_400x300.png)
Category tile illustration for Snacks — flat-style chips bag and mixed snack items (cat-snacks.dim_400x300.png)
App logo mark — a simple, modern leaf-and-cart icon in green tones (logo-icon.dim_128x128.png)
